perturbation.original_text: 2. Confidential Information 2.1 Confidential Information shall mean: i) all technical, financial and business information disclosed by one Party to the other Party or by another Haldex Group entity to the Supplier including, but not limited to, documents, data, information, prototypes or components relating to devices, methods, materials, apparatus, d esigns, research, yields and specifications; and ii) information relating to the existence of this Agreement; 2.2 Confidential Information shall not include: i) information which was known by a Party prior to receiving it from the other Party if this can be demons trated by written record; ii) information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain or which is published after disclosure or otherwise becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement; iii) information which the Party can show was received from a third party who did not to the best knowledge of the Party receive the information, directly or indirectly, from the other Party under any obligation of confidentiality; DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 iv) information developed or created by one Party independently of the other, without any part thereof having been developed or created with assistance or information received from the other Party; and v) information which is disclosed due to any requirement under mandatory law.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Purpose Haldex and the Supplier will evaluate the possibility to enter into an agreement regarding . For this purpose, the Parties need to exchange Confidential Information. 2. Confidential Information 2.1 Confidential Information shall mean: i) all technical, financial and business information disclosed by one Party to the other Party or by another Haldex Group entity to the Supplier including, but not limited to, documents, data, information, prototypes or components relating to devices, methods, materials, apparatus, d esigns, research, yields and specifications; and ii) information relating to the existence of this Agreement; v) information which is disclosed due to any requirement under mandatory law. 2.2 Confidential Information shall not include: i) information which was known by a Party prior to receiving it from the other Party if this can be demons trated by written record; ii) information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain or which is published after disclosure or otherwise becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement; iii) information which the Party can show was received from a third party who did not to the best knowledge of the Party receive the information, directly or indirectly, from the other Party under any obligation of confidentiality; DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 iv) information developed or created by one Party independently of the other, without any part thereof having been developed or created with assistance or information received from the other Party;
perturbation.explanation: By moving 'v) information which is disclosed due to any requirement under mandatory law' into the initial definition of Confidential Information, while it is actually meant to be an *exception*, creates an ambiguity. This is because it could now be interpreted that mandatory disclosures are still considered confidential, undermining the actual intent that such disclosures are exempt. This weakens the 'Disclosure under mandatory law' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Swedish Trade Secrets Act (Lag om skydd för företagshemligheter) § 4
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 4 of the Swedish Trade Secrets Act protects disclosures required by law. Moving the 'mandatory law disclosure' clause into the definition of confidential information creates confusion about whether such legally compelled disclosures are actually protected or still subject to confidentiality, potentially violating the spirit of the law.

perturbation.justification: The original contract clearly defines exceptions to confidential information. The changed contract moves the 'mandatory law disclosure' clause into the definition of confidential information, potentially making legally compelled disclosures still subject to confidentiality. The Law Explanation states this action 'creates confusion about whether such legally compelled disclosures are actually protected or still subject to confidentiality, potentially violating the spirit of the law.' This contradiction is clear because it alters the fundamental understanding of what information is and isn't confidential under the agreement, especially concerning legally mandated disclosures.
